sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Mallorca have struggled recently, winning only 1 of their last 5 matches, with a 3-2 victory over Athletic Club as their highlight, but conceding in all 5 games. Sevilla, on the other hand, have shown mixed results, securing a 2-1 win against Athletic Club but losing 0-3 to Levante UD at home. Their attacking output remains inconsistent, though they’ve managed to score in 3 of their last 5 matches.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Mallorca have the upper hand, with a notable 3-1 away win against Sevilla in October 2025 and a 1-1 draw earlier in February 2025. Mallorca have not lost to Sevilla in their last 3 meetings, which could boost their confidence going into this match.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Stats
sections.text: Mallorca’s Vedat Muriqi is in fine form, scoring 5 goals in the last 5 matches, and will be a major threat up front with his 14 goals this season. Sevilla’s Akor Adams, with 4 goals in recent games, remains their key outlet despite missing players like Alexis Sánchez. Both teams struggle defensively, with Mallorca conceding 33 goals and Sevilla 33 in the season so far.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Mallorca’s strength lies in aerial duels (52.1% win rate) and late scoring (24% of goals after the 76th minute), which could be crucial if the game is tight. Sevilla often score late as well (33% of goals in the last 15 minutes) but have conceded first in their last 6 matches, exposing a vulnerability that Mallorca might exploit.

sections.subtitle: Season Context and Motivation
sections.text: Sitting 18th with 21 points, Mallorca are in desperate need of points to climb out of the relegation zone, making this home game critical. Sevilla, at 14th with 24 points, are slightly better positioned but still under pressure to avoid slipping further. Their slightly superior attacking stats and Sofascore’s 42% away win prediction give them a marginal edge.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Sevilla’s slightly better form and attacking threat, coupled with Mallorca’s defensive frailties (no clean sheet in 7 games), an away win seems plausible but not certain. However, Mallorca’s home advantage and recent head-to-head dominance could lead to a closely contested match, likely with goals on both sides. A narrow Sevilla victory or a draw feels most likely.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Mallorca and Sevilla at Estadi de Son Moix ended in a 1-1 stalemate. Both teams found the back of the net, with Mallorca striking first through a penalty converted by Vedat Muriqi in the 26th minute. Sevilla responded late in the half with a well-taken goal by Neal Maupay just before the break. The game has been competitive, with both sides showing moments of quality and areas for improvement.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance comparison
halftimeSections.text: Sevilla dominated possession and created more entries into the final third and penalty area compared to Mallorca. They also had more shots on target and earned more corner kicks, indicating a stronger attacking presence. However, their expected goals were significantly lower than Mallorca's, suggesting they struggled to convert dominance into high-quality chances. Mallorca, on the other hand, made the most of their limited possession, capitalizing on a penalty and showing efficiency in attack. Defensively, Mallorca were stronger in aerial duels, while Sevilla won more overall duels.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Sevilla largely controlling the attacking momentum, especially in the opening minutes with consistent pressure on Mallorca's defense. Their persistence paid off with a late attacking spell that resulted in the equalizer. Mallorca had fewer but impactful moments of attacking intent, with a notable surge around the time of their penalty goal. The momentum shifted sporadically, but Sevilla maintained more frequent spells of attacking play throughout the half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players and contributions
halftimeSections.text: For Mallorca, Vedat Muriqi stood out as the key figure, not only for scoring the penalty but also for his involvement in creating danger upfront. Jan Virgili also impressed with a strong performance on the ball. On Sevilla's side, Neal Maupay was pivotal with his clinical finish to level the score, while Gabriel Suazo contributed significantly in creating opportunities. Both teams will look to these players to inspire their attacks in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Areas of concern and opportunity
halftimeSections.text: Mallorca will need to address their lack of possession and limited final third entries to gain more control in the game. Their defense, however, has held firm in key moments, which could be a foundation to build on. Sevilla must focus on converting their territorial dominance into better goal-scoring opportunities, as they missed a big chance in the first half. Their higher duel-winning rate could be an asset if they continue to press high up the pitch.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Given Sevilla's superior possession and attacking entries, they are likely to push for a winning goal in the second half, especially if they can capitalize on their chances. Mallorca, however, have shown they can be dangerous on the counter or from set pieces, and their efficiency could see them steal another goal. Both teams are expected to tighten up defensively, but there’s potential for more goals if either side exploits the other’s weaknesses.
